executiveSummary: Iteration 000031 fully implements queue recording and per-entry deletion at the functional level. Sequential playback chaining, automatic stop on last entry, manual stop, and accessible delete controls all work as specified. Two functional requirements have gaps: FR-6 does not comply (delete button is rendered even for 'generating' entries, contra the PRD restriction); FR-5 partially complies (handleDeleteQueueEntry calls stopRecording() directly instead of delegating to handleStopQueueRecording(), leaving playingEntryId uncleared and the video un-paused when a non-playing entry is deleted during queue recording). All four user stories are functionally satisfied, but each carries a 'partially_comply' rating because their typecheck/lint acceptance criteria (US-001-AC07, US-002-AC04, US-003-AC04, US-004-AC06) are unmet — the repository currently has 115 lint errors and 4 typecheck errors. These failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this iteration's code, but the ACs require the gate to pass unconditionally.
minorObservations: FR-6 non-compliance detail: the delete button is rendered for ALL non-completed entries via the '!isCompleted' branch (App.tsx:1429-1440), which includes status='generating'. The fix is to add an '!isGenerating' guard so the button only appears for 'queued' and 'failed' entries.

FR-5 partial gap: handleDeleteQueueEntry (App.tsx:760) calls stopRecording() directly rather than handleStopQueueRecording(). When the deleted entry is NOT the currently playing entry, the video continues playing and playingEntryId remains set after recording stops. Routing through handleStopQueueRecording() (or adding video.pause() + setPlayingEntryId(null) after stopRecording()) would fully align with the spec.

Quality gate: npm run typecheck reports 4 errors (in use-agent-generation.test.ts, use-agent-parameters.test.ts, mcp/parameter-store.test.ts — all pre-existing, none in iteration code). npm run lint reports 115 errors (pre-existing). These block AC07/AC04/AC06 across all four user stories.

A11y: icon-only delete buttons (showing '×') rely entirely on aria-label. On touch devices with no hover tooltip, users cannot discover the label without activating the button. Adding visible helper text or a tooltip on focus would improve discoverability.

UX: the visual distinction between 'Record Queue' (circle indicator, rounded-full) and 'Stop Recording' (square indicator, rounded-sm) is appropriate — circle-to-record and square-to-stop is a standard media convention. No change needed.

Performance: per-row handlers (handleDeleteQueueEntry, handlePlayQueueEntry) are recreated on each render. Acceptable for current list sizes; memoized row components would reduce work if queues grow large.
The 'Record Queue' button's disable guard adds isRecording beyond FR-7's spec (which only cites isFinalizing and no completed entries). This is a safe, conservative addition.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: Two concrete fixes are recommended before closing the iteration: (1) FR-6 — hide the delete button for status='generating' entries by adding '!isGenerating' to the conditional at App.tsx:1429; (2) FR-5 — in handleDeleteQueueEntry, replace the direct stopRecording() + setIsQueueRecordingActive(false) block with a call to handleStopQueueRecording() so that video pause and playingEntryId reset are included. Additionally, the pre-existing typecheck and lint errors should be remediated (or the ACs scoped to iteration-introduced errors) so that the quality gates are meaningful. All other acceptance criteria are satisfied; no architectural changes are required.
